codeReview.summary: All 10 VAL-AUTH assertions (VAL-AUTH-001 through VAL-AUTH-010) are covered by 16 passing tests. Tests cover login flows, registration flows, logout flows, session persistence, and UI/edge cases. Implementation follows AGENTS.md conventions: role-based selectors, networkidle waits, unique usernames, proper cleanup.

issues.description: VAL-AUTH-010 test has a conditional `if (isOnLogin)` block that makes URL and heading assertions optional. If the invalid token doesn't trigger redirect, the test still passes because only the localStorage clearing is asserted unconditionally. This reduces test precision - a proper redirect should be asserted. However, the token clearing check ensures the core behavior is validated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff references commitId '65ec433' (a schema fix for Profile.bio) but the actual test implementation is in commit '51af3ee'. This commit ID mismatch could confuse future reviewers. The worker correctly implemented the feature in 51af3ee, but the handoff metadata may have been generated incorrectly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commitId field shows '65ec433' but e2e/auth-comprehensive.spec.ts was added in commit 51af3ee. The handoff itself correctly describes the auth tests work.

summary: Feature e2e-auth-comprehensive implemented correctly with comprehensive coverage of all 10 VAL-AUTH assertions (VAL-AUTH-001 through VAL-AUTH-010) plus additional UI and edge case tests. All tests pass consistently across multiple runs. One minor issue: VAL-AUTH-010 has conditional assertions that reduce precision, but core behavior (token clearing) is still validated. Implementation follows project conventions.
